claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 4 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for your help. I'm looking to claim for my two children. I have Alex who is 6 years old, and Blake who is 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's lovely to meet you. Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex (6) and Blake (4).

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Are you responsible for both Alex and Blake? For example, do they live with you, or do you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care (such as food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex lives with me at home. But Blake doesn't live with me - he lives with his other parent. I do help out with some money for Blake though, you know, for clothes and bits and pieces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. It's helpful to know the different living arrangements for each child.

Regarding Blake, who lives with his other parent - could you tell me roughly how much you contribute towards Blake's care each week or month? 

For reference, to continue receiving Child Benefit for a child who lives with someone else, you generally need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (currently £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child, or £16.95 per week for additional children).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I give about £20 a week for Blake - for things like clothes and school bits when needed.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that detail. £20 per week would meet the threshold for contributing towards Blake's upkeep.

Just a couple more questions:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know, thank you.

One last question: Are either Alex or Blake in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, or are there any other special circumstances I should be aware of (such as fostering arrangements or local council involvement in their care)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. They're both just living normal lives - Alex here with me and Blake with his other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

---

## Eligibility Outcome

### ✅ Alex (age 6) - **Eligible**
Alex meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for their care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

### ✅ Blake (age 4) - **Eligible**
Even though Blake lives with his other parent, you can still claim Child Benefit because you contribute £20 per week towards Blake's upkeep. This exceeds the minimum contribution threshold of £16.95 per week (the rate for an additional child). Your contributions towards clothes and school items count as contributing to the child's care.

---

## Summary
You appear to be eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake. 

**A few things to keep in mind:**
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, so please ensure Blake's other parent is not also claiming for Blake. If there's any disagreement about who should claim, HMRC can help resolve this.
- If your individual income (or your partner's) is over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge.
- If your circumstances change (such as the amount you contribute to Blake's care), you should notify the Child Benefit Office.

I hope this helps! Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances such as hospital stays or care arrangements. Alex meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 4 years old (under 16) and lives with his other parent. However, the claimant contributes £20 per week towards Blake's upkeep, which exceeds the minimum threshold of £16.95 per week (the rate for an additional child). This contribution towards clothes and school items counts as contributing to the child's upkeep. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and there are no special circumstances. Under the rules for children who live with someone else, the claimant can continue to receive Child Benefit as they are making sufficient contributions to Blake's care.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for two children. Alex (6) lives with the claimant directly, meeting the standard eligibility criteria. Blake (4) lives with his other parent, but the claimant contributes £20 per week towards his upkeep, which exceeds the minimum contribution threshold required to maintain eligibility for a child living elsewhere. Neither child has another person claiming Child Benefit for them, and there are no complicating factors such as hospital stays or care arrangements. The claimant appears eligible for Child Benefit for both children.
